content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Pascal compiler (p24p) for COR24 targeting the pv24a p-code VM. C compiler via tc24r for the compiler itself, Pascal runtime written in Pascal (dogfooded). Phase 0: expression language compiler in C (integers, booleans, assignment, if/while, writeln). Phase 1: block structure compiler in C (procedures, functions, arrays, records, var params, nested scopes, for/repeat/case, read/readln). Phase 1 runtime: rewrite runtime in Pascal (I/O formatting, standard functions, diagnostics). Pipeline: .pas → p24p (C, native) → .spc → pasm → .p24 → pv24a VM.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-pascal-subset-spec [production]: Define the Phase 0 Pascal subset grammar and .spc emission spec. Create docs/...
  [x] 002-002-lexer [legacy]: Implement the Phase 0 lexer in C. Create src/lexer.c and src/lexer.h. The lex... <-- YOU ARE HERE

=== CURRENT STEP: 002-002-lexer [legacy] ===
Status: completed
Description: Implement the Phase 0 lexer in C. Create src/lexer.c and src/lexer.h. The lex...

=== PROMPT ===
Implement the Phase 0 lexer in C. Create src/lexer.c and src/lexer.h. The lexer tokenizes Pascal source from a char buffer (not file I/O — tc24r has no fopen). Implement: all tokens from docs/pascal-subset.md (keywords case-insensitive, identifiers, integer literals, symbols including two-char operators like := <> <= >=, comments { } and (* *)). Use a simple state machine. Provide a next_token() interface that returns token type, lexeme string, and line number. Include a test program (tests/test_lexer.c) that tokenizes a small Pascal program and prints each token. Verify it compiles with tc24r and runs on cor24-run.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)
